message:
fix(fcp-core): simplify CRDT LWW merge predicate to correctly handle equal-timestamp entries

The previous merge condition `existing.wins_over(entry) || existing == entry`
had a subtle flaw: when two entries are equal (same timestamp, same value),
the condition evaluated to true and skipped the insert, which is correct
behavior but expressed the intent poorly and masked an edge case where
`wins_over` returns false for both directions (a true tie with different
values but identical timestamps).

The simplified predicate `!entry.wins_over(existing)` is logically equivalent
for the common case but correctly handles the tie-breaking semantics: if the
incoming entry doesn't strictly win, keep the existing one. This makes the
merge idempotent and commutative without relying on equality as a separate
branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
